concerns.text: The plan assumes a 'simulated/generated dataset' or 'manual upload' will be available to satisfy FR-001 and the research question. However, the research question requires specific empirical variables (cold work %, alloy composition, time-to-peak softening) to validate the 'pinning hypothesis'. A simulated dataset, by definition, cannot validate a physical hypothesis unless the simulation parameters are derived from a verified, real-world dataset which is currently missing. Relying on synthetic data for the primary validation of a materials science claim renders the methodology incapable of answering the research question as stated.

The plan explicitly states: 'None of these verified sources contain the required materials science variables... Consequently, the implementation plan includes a critical dependency on a simulated/generated dataset'. This confirms that no real, accessible source exists for the required data within the project's constraints. Without a real dataset, the 'Verified Accuracy' and 'Reproducibility' principles of the Constitution cannot be met for the core research claim, as the results will be artifacts of the simulation logic rather than empirical findings.
